Ad Text:
Associate, Corporate Finance & Strategy | Avalon Beach, NSW (Hybrid)

 

About the Role

This is a high-impact finance role at the centre of a growing global hospitality group.

As Associate, Corporate Finance & Strategy, you'll work across financial planning, analysis, and strategic initiatives partnering with finance and operational leaders to deliver the forecasting, reporting, and insights that drive decision-making at every level of the business.

From monthly P&L and cash flow forecasting to Board reporting packs and investment analysis, your work will span the full breadth of Group finance. You'll also contribute to strategic projects, covenant tracking, scenario modelling, and continuous improvement including opportunities to lead AI adoption in financial processes.

What you'll do:

Support budgeting and long-range planning across the Group
Deliver monthly P&L, cash flow, and liquidity forecasting
Prepare financial analysis including budget vs. actuals commentary
Build reporting packs for leadership, Board, and investors
Support external reporting, covenant tracking, and funding analysis
Conduct scenario analysis and contribute to financial modelling
Play an active role in strategic planning and investment decisions
Drive process improvement and support AI adoption in finance

 

About You

You're commercially sharp, analytically strong, and comfortable translating financial data into clear, strategic insight. You communicate well across finance and non-finance audiences, bring genuine attention to detail, and thrive in a collaborative, fast-moving environment.

You'll bring:

4+ years’ experience in finance, consulting or related fields
Ideally combines strong technical grounding (e.g. audit, transaction services, consulting) with exposure to commercial or operational finance roles
CA / CPA qualification (highly regarded)
Strong command of financial statements, budgeting, and forecasting
Advanced Excel skills, including financial modelling
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau (highly advantageous)
Sector experience in hospitality or travel (a plus, not a must)

 

About Us

Beckons is built around journeys of discovery for curious travelers seeking deeper connection to place, culture, and the natural world. Through a growing collection of lodges in some of the world’s most rare and remarkable destinations, the brand is guided by a regenerative approach to luxury travel, one that goes beyond sustainability to create positive, lasting impact for landscapes, wildlife, and the communities who call these places home.

 

The Beckons collection includes five award-winning properties in Australia, Southern Ocean Lodge on Kangaroo Island, Longitude 131° at Uluṟu-Kata Tjuṯa, Capella Lodge on Lord Howe Island, Silky Oaks Lodge at the Daintree Rainforest, The Louise in the Barossa Valley, while across the Tasman Sea in New Zealand lies the century-young Huka Lodge in Taupō. In the Americas, lodges include Clayoquot Wilderness Lodge on Canada’s Vancouver Island, and the wilds of Tierra Atacama and Tierra Patagonia in Chile.

Job Title: RFQ EL1 IM Policy, Governance & Compliance Lead
Ad Text:
Your new company

You'll be joining a specialist consulting environment working with a large, complex Australian Government agency in a data‑intensive context. The organisation is undertaking a significant uplift in its information and data management maturity, with a strong focus on legislative compliance, digital continuity and practical, staff‑friendly governance. This work sits at the intersection of policy, technology and operational reality, and is sponsored at senior executive level.

Your new role

As a senior Information Management / Records Management specialist, you will lead the development of contemporary, APS‑aligned IM policy and compliance artefacts and provide expert advice on records management capability across Microsoft 365.



Key responsibilities include:

Reviewing existing IM policy artefacts and conducting a structured gap analysis, including IM Policy, Normal Administrative Practice Policy, Digitise to Dispose Policy, IM Governance Framework and IM Strategy and Delivery Plan
Designing and authoring a new Information and Data Archival Policy and associated governance artefacts from first principles, aligned to the Archives Act 1983 and Privacy Act 1988
Producing clear, practical, plain‑English staff guidance for managing information in Outlook, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, network drives and Microsoft Purview
Ensuring all artefacts align with the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF, NAA Digital Continuity 2025 and relevant APS standards
Facilitating policy review and endorsement sessions with governance, legal, enterprise architecture and cyber security stakeholders
Assessing SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview against NAA records management requirements to identify gaps, risks and remediation options
Engaging with ICT Solution Front Door processes and IM, architecture, security and operations teams to validate technology feasibility
Delivering a structured Records Management Compliance Assessment Report with risk‑rated findings and recommendations
Contributing IM policy and compliance expertise to pilot lessons‑learned and compliance monitoring frameworks

What you'll need to succeed


To be successful in this role, you will bring:

7+ years' experience in APS information management, records management or IM compliance roles, ideally in health, statistics or other data‑intensive environments
Deep working knowledge of the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF and NAA Digital Continuity 2025
Demonstrated experience authoring APS‑standard policies, governance frameworks and practical staff guidance that is genuinely adopted
Proven ability to conduct formal IM or records management compliance assessments with risk‑rated findings
Strong written communication skills, with experience producing SES‑facing material independently
Hands‑on understanding of SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview from a governance and records compliance perspective
Familiarity with Microsoft Purview retention and disposition capabilities
Experience engaging with APS IT governance and Solution Front Door‑style architecture review processes
Awareness of emerging AI and Microsoft Copilot governance considerations for information management
Relevant qualifications in records management, information management, library science, law or a related discipline (desirable)

What you'll get in return

You'll have the opportunity to work on high-impact, nationally significant IM initiatives, with strong executive sponsorship and access to senior stakeholders. This role offers a chance to shape enduring policy and governance foundations, influence technology decisions, and apply best-practice information management in a modern Microsoft 365 environment.

What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or email kylie.garcia@hay.com.au.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

About the West Region Delivery Team

The West Region Delivery Team (WRDT) is delivering one of Sydney Water’s most significant infrastructure programs, with a circa $2bn pipeline of works over 10 years.

Operating in a collaborative, alliance-style environment, WRDT brings together delivery partners to plan and deliver critical water infrastructure that supports the growth of Greater Sydney.

About the opportunity

We are seeking a Data and Reporting Analyst to support the delivery of the WRDT program. Working closely with delivery, commercial and program leadership teams, you will interpret business requirements and translate them into meaningful reporting that improves visibility, supports decision making and drives better outcomes. Day to day you will;

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports that provide clear, accurate and timely insights across the program.

Analyse data across cost, schedule, risk and delivery performance to identify trends, risks and opportunities.

Translate business requirements into structured, practical reporting outputs that support both operational and executive decision making.

Support the preparation of high-quality reports, presentations and executive-level slide decks.

Work closely with stakeholders to improve how data is captured, structured and used across the program.

Identify opportunities to streamline reporting processes and improve efficiency, consistency and usability.

Contribute to improving data quality and reporting frameworks across the program.

What you’ll bring

Strong experience in data analysis and reporting within a complex project or infrastructure environment.

Advanced Power BI capability, including dashboard development and data modelling.

Ability to interpret data and provide clear, practical insights, not just produce reports.

Strong commercial awareness, with an understanding of how data supports delivery outcomes.

Ability to engage with stakeholders and translate ambiguous requirements into structured outputs.

High attention to detail and ability to present information clearly and professionally.

Strong communication skills, with experience preparing executive-level presentations and reports.

Why join us

Be part of a long-term, high-profile infrastructure program with real impact.

A collaborative and supportive team environment.

Opportunities to grow and develop a long term career.

Interested? Apply Now!

If this sounds like you then APPLY NOW or reach out to Hayley Urriola for more information.

Job Title: Expression of Interest - Executive Advisor - Commercial, SA
Ad Text:
At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

Don’t let our size worry you. Although we are a large firm supporting 12,000+ employees across the world, we are an Australian company approaching 100 years in operation that is 100% employee owned. Our size ensures we have access to world-class talent, but our ownership structure ensures authentic and agile project delivery by local experts. 

Our GHD’s Infrastructure Advisory practice, a team of > 400 people across ANZ, South-east Asia and the Pacific, supports Government, asset managers and infrastructure investors who make informed decisions about capital deployment, operational optimisation and liability transformation across every industry sector.

Who are we looking for?

We are looking for Executive Advisors who are familiar with business case development, economic / financial modelling, project advisory, major project procurement modelling and strategy / regulation as we expand our Commercial Advisory team in SA.

Ideally based in Adelaide Office, your role will be to lead key Federal Government client relationships and engagements that shape Australia’s infrastructure policy and associated major projects.

Working with a high performing team this position offers a variety of work and will see you involved in:

Using your expertise and knowledge to successfully win, secure and deliver opportunities in business case development, economic modelling public policy and infrastructure strategy development.

Identify key contacts and opportunities and lead client engagement.

Leading project pursuits including internal teaming arrangements and contractual negotiations.

Establishing and maintaining effective relationships with clients and collaborating with internal GHD regions and Leadership teams.

Providing technical leadership on nominated projects as required.

Mentoring and guiding Business Advisory professionals through the initial stages of their career.

What would you bring to the team? 

Experience delivering Commercial centered Business Advisory services. 

Proven experience delivering Commercial business cases, economic and financial modelling and policy development.

You will have proven experience in working across business development, technical and design leadership, client satisfaction and relationship building spanning multi-disciplinary projects. 

Commercial acumen and critical-thinking ability 

Strong report writing, interviewing, facilitation and presentation skills.

Ability to influence others, strong negotiating skills.

Join us on our next phase of growth: GHD Advisory – clear, actionable advice that is anchored in deep technical knowledge and drives our clients’ success.

Interested? Apply now
Contact: Zoe Ferguson - Talent Acquisition Team Lead

GHD is an equal opportunity employer, and we want everyone to be able to fully participate in our recruitment process. We value a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people of all identities.

GHD embraces a range of flexible work practices to create a work environment focused on well-being, flexibility and productivity where our people can thrive. 

Be part of the dynamic culture! Our workplace thrives on a hybrid model, combining in-office collaboration and flexibility. Join us in office, three days a week to contribute, connect and excel in our vibrant environment.

About Us

Workwear Group (WWG) is part of Wesfarmers Industrial & Safety a division of Wesfarmers, a top 20 ASX listed company with over 100,000 employees. Housed under the banner of Workwear Group, our three iconic brands - KingGee, Hard Yakka and NNT - have been in the business of delivering workwear since the late 1920's. 

We are the trusted partner in workwear solutions, manufacturing over seven million units each year. What sets us apart is our continuous investment in innovation, market knowledge, pioneering ideas and sustainability practices.

We work as one team. We put our customers first and are passionate about what we do. Joining WWG is an opportunity to thrive in an inspiring, safe, and inclusive community.

About the Role

As a Senior Integration Engineer, you will design, deliver, and maintain robust integration solutions that connect internal and external applications, with a strong focus on B2C and B2B e-commerce platforms. Leveraging Workato as the primary integration platform, alongside Microsoft Azure and other modern technologies, you’ll ensure seamless data flow, security, and scalability across critical business systems.

You’ll work closely with Product Owners, engineering teams, and business stakeholders to translate requirements into secure, high-performing solutions. This role demands expertise in API development, data transformation, and cloud integration, combined with strong problem-solving skills to optimize and troubleshoot complex integrations. Your work will underpin the digital backbone of our operations, enabling innovation and exceptional customer experiences.

What you’ll be doing

Design, develop, and maintain integration solutions using Workato and Azure technologies to connect internal and external systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders and technical teams to gather requirements and translate them into secure, scalable integration designs.
Build and manage APIs, ensuring compliance with authentication protocols and data security standards.
Monitor and optimize existing integrations for performance, reliability, and scalability.
Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues, including root cause analysis and implementing long-term fixes.
Ensure all incidents and service requests are resolved within agreed SLAs, maintaining clear communication with stakeholders.
Implement ETL processes and data transformation workflows to support accurate and efficient data exchange.
Participate in architecture reviews and present integration designs for approval and alignment with enterprise standards.
Support CI/CD pipelines and version control practices to enable continuous improvement and rapid deployment of integration solutions.
Provide after-hours support when required and maintain disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity.

Who we’re looking for

At our company, we value not only our great brands but also the people and teams that drive our success. In addition to technical skills, we seek team members who possess the following personable qualities:

Strong work ethic and are committed to showing up for a team
Openness to receive coaching and embrace self reflection and self growth
Accountability for your actions, and emotions, and actively venture beyond your comfort zone
Embrace innovation, take calculated risks, and inspire others to think outside the box
Ability to build meaningful relationships, we prioritise our customers. Balancing commercial goals with customer satisfaction is key
Ethical standards and demonstrating honesty and integrity in all interactions is fundamental to our team culture.

What we’re looking for

Proven experience designing and delivering integrations using Workato (working knowledge required) and Azure Logic Apps.
Strong understanding of API development and management, including RESTful and SOAP services, authentication protocols (OAuth, JWT), and secure data exchange.
Proficiency in programming and scripting languages such as Java, Python, Ruby, or JavaScript for automation and data transformation.
Hands-on experience with ETL processes, data mapping, and working with formats like XML, JSON, and SQL.
Solid knowledge of cloud integration principles, particularly Azure services, and familiarity with security controls and compliance frameworks.
Ability to troubleshoot, optimize, and maintain integration solutions, ensuring scalability, reliability, and performance.

What we offer

The chance to join a great team of like-minded, hard-working people who are passionate about achieving our vision and the growth objectives of the company.
Flexible working environment, allowing for work from home day/s each week.
A competitive salary package and suite of benefits and perks, including a 50% discount on our brands Hard Yakka, KingGee and NNT and 5% Wesfarmers discount for Kmart, Bunnings, Officeworks and more.
A tailored career and development plan, plus reward and recognition programs. Whatever your goals we’re here to support you on that journey.
Our Wellbeing program, Thrive, with activities designed to support the Financial Wellness of our team, Celebrate Diversity, ensure we Get Physical and maintain Healthy Minds, including challenges, yoga, meditation and access to our Employee Assistance Program.
We Learn, a dedicated hub for all things learning and development.

We actively work on creating a safe and inclusive environment free of discrimination. At Workwear Group we aspire to build a culture where diversity is celebrated, where Team Members feel a sense of “I Belong” and are empowered to bring their whole selves to work.

You can follow us on LinkedIn or see our company careers page for more opportunities https://www.workweargroup.com.au/careers

​Thank you for your interest in Workwear Group. Due to anticipated high volumes of applicants, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Job Title: Senior Automation Engineer | Identity Platform | Finance
Ad Text:
Our client's Directory Services team manages a large-scale hybrid identity platform and they need an engineer who writes the code that makes it run itself.

The identity context can be learned. The automation fundamentals can't. If you're strong in Terraform and Python and you solve problems with code, this role is worth a conversation.

What you'll be doing:

You'll be building and maintaining the automation engines that underpin a global identity platform. That means writing code (Python, PowerShell, Terraform) to automate identity lifecycle workflows, deploying and managing infrastructure as code, and building monitoring and reporting solutions to keep the platform healthy and secure. You'll work across a hybrid environment touching Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID, Ping and Google Identity.

What they're looking for:

Strong hands-on Terraform skills
Python proficiency, comfortable building real automation logic, not just scripts
An engineering and developer mindset, you solve problems with code
Experience in cloud or hybrid infrastructure environments
Exposure to identity or access management concepts is a genuine advantage (Entra ID, AD, JML workflows)
Monitoring and reporting experience (Sentinel, Splunk, PowerBI, KQL) valued

Why this role:

Genuine build and engineering scope, not a BAU admin role
Hybrid working in Sydney
Platform that operates at global scale across a critical financial institution
A team that values engineers who build, not just manage

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Cody Berry on 02 8289 3123 for a confidential discussion.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Talent International is searching for an experienced Source 2 pay Business Analyst to join our client, a large healthcare support organisation. This is a 6-month contract role based in Brisbane CBD.

About the role:

The Source to Pay (S2P) Analyst will support the S2P Lead during implementation of the Coupa S2P platform and integration with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance (ERP). This role ensures the design, configuration, testing, and deployment of future-state processes across the complete S2P lifecycle from sourcing through payment.

Key Responsibilities:

Document current state (AS-IS) and design future state (TO-BE) S2P processes including sourcing, contracts, supplier management, requisitioning, purchasing, invoice processing, expense management, and payments in line with compliance and reporting requirements.
Gather detailed requirements from stakeholders interpreting and producing information from multiple sources.
Support and validate Coupa platform configuration across Sourcing, Contracts, Procurement, Invoicing, Expenses, and Pay modules including workflows, approval matrices, catalogues, tax and policy rules.
Support and validate integration requirements ensuring accurate data flows for suppliers, transactions, GL posting, payments and reconciliation.
Support and validate data migration activities including supplier master data, open transactions, contracts, catalogues, and historical transactions with validation and quality checks.
Develop comprehensive test scenarios with System Integration Partners, Taxonomy Leads and Subject Matter Experts.
Execute testing during SIT and UAT covering end-to-end S2P processes and integrations.
Create training materials and user guides for diverse audiences including procurement teams, requesters, approvers, suppliers, employees, and finance users.
Partner with S2P Lead, System Implementation Partners and Integrators to identify and resolve design gaps, integration issues, and process inefficiencies.
Contribute to cutover planning and support go-live activities including reconciliations, issue resolution, and hypercare.

Skills :

Strong knowledge of end-to-end Source to Pay processes including sourcing, contracts, supplier management, procurement, invoicing, expenses, and payments.
3-5 years business analysis experience, preferably in procurement transformation, ERP implementation, or S2P platform projects.
Ability to gather and interpret complex information from multiple sources, draw the insights together, and clearly and confidently communicate well‑supported, evidence‑based conclusions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail and ability to identify data inconsistencies.
Ability to work with large data sets for reconciliations, data validation, and migration testing.
Proficiency in process mapping tools and creating detailed documentation.
Advanced Excel skills for data analysis, validation, reconciliation, and migration testing.
Ability to think outside the box with a future vision for transformation and continuous improvement.
Effective communicator able to work with Procurement, Accounts Payable, Finance, IT, Suppliers, and external implementation partners.

How to Apply:

To find out more, please "Apply for this job" or contact Jenifer Mitchell on 0414 870 046 or jenifer.mitchell@talentinternational.com

For a list of all vacant positions, please see our website www.talentinternational.com

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Talent International is searching for an experienced Delivery Lead to join our client, a large healthcare support organisation. This is a 6-month contract role based in Brisbane CBD.

About the role:
The Delivery Lead will be responsible for driving the delivery of assigned workstreams and associated supplier contracts for a large-scale ERP Finance & Operations project that includes significant Chart of Accounts changes to existing ERP systems (e.g. HCM, CRM) and new supply chain components (Coupa). The role focuses on ensuring that contract deliverables associated with the various supplier agreements are executed in line with program timelines and obligations. This is a delivery-focused role, providing oversight over subject matter expert activities, vendor accountability, and coordination of internal dependencies. The position is part of a combined project leadership team, reporting to the Project Director.

Key Responsibilities:

Drive the delivery of assigned supplier contracts and workstreams, ensuring obligations are met on time and to the required quality.
Facilitate task planning, status reporting and reviews at a practical level, ensuring clarity of scope and pace of delivery.
Consult closely with the project's Cutover Management and data streams to determine the most effective and risk-managed approach to implemented need changes to Third Party Applications (ERPs) to allow the new Finance & Operations system to go live.
Manage and work with a specialist implementation partner (in concert with the project's organisational change management stream) to introduce major enhancements / process changes to the supply chain solution based on Coupa.
Monitor progress, track deliverables, and proactively address risks, issues, and blockers within the assigned scope.
Hold suppliers accountable for delivery quality and adherence to agreed contractual commitments.
Manage and coordinate dependencies within the workstream and across related streams.
Provide clear, transparent reporting on delivery progress, highlighting risks to scope, schedule, or quality.
Ensure delivery practices align with enterprise and project governance standards while maintaining Agile ways of working.


Skills & Experience:

Significant experience in leading delivery of IT vendor contracts or workstreams within large-scale ERP and/or enterprise transformation programs.
Experience with ERP systems and supply chain implementations.
Experience with large-scale finance system migrations highly desirable.
Experience with procurement and supply chain systems implementation, including components such as contracts management, purchasing, and expense management highly desirable.
Familiarity with Agile ways of working and practical experience in task planning, backlog management, and reporting.
Strong vendor management and stakeholder engagement skills, with proven ability to hold external partners accountable to obligations.
Experience with delivery tools such as Jira and Atlassian desirable.
Proven ability to manage risks, issues, and dependencies within complex multi-vendor environments.
Excellent problem-solving, facilitation, and communication skills.
Relevant certifications in Project Management disciplines (including Agile) or equivalent demonstrated experience.
Healthcare sector experience is desirable, although not essential.


How to Apply:
To find out more, please "Apply for this job" or contact Jenifer Mitchell on 0414 870 046 or jenifer.mitchell@talentinternational.com

About Us

At Sportsbet, we’re united behind our purpose to Bring Excitement to Life for over 2.4 million customers. We do this by delivering fun, challenging the status quo, and living our values. As part of the global Flutter Group, we’re a major player internationally, but in Melbourne, Sydney and Darwin, we’re a tightknit team of 1,300+ teammates (with over half in Tech & Data Science).

Our commitment to responsible gambling is genuine and demonstrated through the wide range of responsible gambling initiatives and tools. We’re proud to be the leaders in responsible gambling in our industry, not just locally, but globally.


We are one of the top workplaces for Women through WORK180. WORK180 is a leading platform that independently assesses employers on policies, benefits and practices that support women and underrepresented groups at work. We remain committed to making Sportsbet the best place to work and encourage you to explore our employer profile on the WORK180 website to learn more about what we offer. 

About The Role

Reporting to the Data Science Lead, you will collaborate with stakeholders to align machine learning and other quantitative models with strategic business objectives, ensuring impactful results. You will design, build, and deploy models using sophisticated deep learning and machine learning methods to address diverse business challenges.

Your role involves translating complex technical concepts into actionable insights for various audiences, presenting findings both internally and externally. You will apply MLOps principles to maintain high standards in coding, model evaluation, and quality assurance.

As a part of cross-functional teams, you will work with technical, commercial and risk & compliance stakeholders to deliver sustainable data science products. You will be proactive in managing and communicating project timelines and mitigating risks.

About You

While we are big on development and offer on the job training, we want to set you up for success. Along with a great attitude, it would be ideal that you have:

Industry experience as a mid-level Data Scientist
Proven experience with production-level ML models
Experience working with and influencing commercial stakeholders

Additionally, it would be highly regarded if you have:

Experience with production-grade recommendation systems
Experience with production-grade AI agents for Data Science or MLOps
Ability to mentor junior team members


The Perks

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus for permanent staff members, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, an additional week’s leave!)
Hybrid working policy (Monday and Friday from home) and a $1,050 home office allowance for new starters.
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.
Hack Days - where your creativity and collaborative ideas lead to exciting new ideas and projects for our customers.
Access to outstanding career development programs, including expert-led internal training and external learning opportunities (staff allowance for personal development)
Weekly concierge service including free massages and nail appointments – all onsite in our Melbourne office!
Free breakfast, daily fresh fruit and weekly rotating socials, from Thursday office drinks to fun team events
End of trip facilities with onsite table tennis and pool tables
Discounted health insurance and free flu jabs yearly.

We review applications within the first two weeks, so apply now! Check out our Life@Sportsbet Instagram, LinkedIn, and careers website for more information about our inclusive culture.  

Our Focus on Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

We don’t just support diversity – we celebrate it! This is a workplace where you can thrive and feel safe to bring your whole authentic self to work.

This is a permanent role available in QLD or NSW with hybrid and flexible working arrangements.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a newly created senior leadership role with genuine end-to-end ownership. You will define and deliver the data, analytics and responsible AI agenda—shaping how decisions are made, how leaders operate, and how the business grows. Backed by strong executive sponsorship and a clear transformation mandate, this role offers the chance to turn data into a strategic asset with organisation-wide impact.

About the Role

Reporting to the EGM, Digital and Data Transformation, the Head of Data Insights is responsible for defining and delivering the organisation’s data, analytics and AI strategy. The role combines strategic leadership with hands-on delivery and enterprise influence.

Key Responsibilities

Define and execute the enterprise Data & Insights strategy aligned to the 5-year transformation roadmap

Design and deliver a modern enterprise data platform (warehouse or lakehouse)

Establish and embed data governance, ownership, quality, retention and destruction policies

Lead responsible adoption of AI and automation across the organisation

Partner with senior leaders to translate data capability into commercial outcomes

Build, lead and develop a high-performing Data & AI team

About You

You are an experienced data leader with strong executive presence and the ability to influence change in complex environments.

 You will bring:

8+ years’ experience leading data, analytics or information management functions

A proven background delivering enterprise data platform or warehouse transformations

Strong expertise in data governance, privacy, security and regulatory compliance

Solid technical foundations across data architecture, analytics and cloud platforms

Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills

 Highly regarded experience:

Retail, automotive or customer‑centric industries

Scaling automation across AI/ML, RPA and process redesign

Tools such as Power BI and modern data orchestration platforms

Why Join MotorCycle Holdings?

Greenfield opportunity to shape enterprise data and AI capability

Executive‑level impact with direct access to senior leadership

Hybrid working with flexibility across NSW or QLD

Join a values‑led organisation that prioritises accountability, excellence, integrity and collaboration

Ready to Make an Impact?

If you’re looking for a career‑defining role where you can shape strategy, build capability and see your work influence real business outcomes, we’d love to hear from you.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

As a Senior Strategy Manager at SEEK, you'll play a pivotal role in defining and delivering our long-term vision and growth ambitions. Focused on topics within SEEK's APAC product strategy as part of the Strategy Operations Centre of Gravity, you'll tackle some of our most critical and complex challenges, spanning multiple product and AI domains.

You'll lead deep strategic thinking, apply analytical rigour, and turn insights into action. This role suits someone who thrives in ambiguity, is energised by solving complex business problems, and is excited by the opportunity to shape SEEK's future at scale.

What You'll Be Doing

✅ Lead strategy projects spanning multiple product and AI domains, becoming the 'go-to' expert for senior leadership and the broader organisation
✅ Develop and apply analytical frameworks to investigate business issues, identify solutions and make recommendations
✅ Create situational awareness across the organisation through strategic metrics, key results, industry trends and competitor analysis
✅ Communicate strategy with simplicity and impact to all levels—from senior executives to delivery teams
✅ Collaborate in project teams alongside Product, AI and Technology stakeholders to design, test and iterate future solutions
✅ Be a vocal advocate for the strategy, providing guidance to execution teams to ensure alignment

The Opportunity

You'll join SEEK's global Strategy team, a high-performing group of ~40 ex-consultants from premier firms, working across APAC.  With exposure to some of SEEK's most senior leaders, including the Head of Strategy Operations, Directors of Strategy, and Group Executives of Product, AI and Technology, you'll have a rare opportunity to shape the long-term direction of one of the region's most recognised tech brands. Our team culture is caring, curious and impact-oriented, and we're united by a shared purpose: helping people live more fulfilling and productive working lives.

Qualifications

✔ 5-8 years' experience in strategy-related roles, with 3-4 years in a top-tier strategy consulting firm
✔ Highly developed conceptual reasoning, analytical and problem-solving skills with proven ability to frame strategic problems
✔ Experience in developing business-level strategy and breaking this down into objectives, targets and initiatives
✔ Track record of delivering results through cross-functional teams
✔ Ability to influence and build support for fact-based recommendations with diverse stakeholders
✔ High learning orientation and desire to become a global expert in online marketplace design and operations
✔ Passion for growth strategy and excellent business acumen, with experience leading complex and ambiguous strategic projects
✔ Experience in media, internet or tech industries is a plus

Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Free income protection insurance

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Role Summary

Resmed’s Global Supply Chain Planning ambition is to be a trusted partner orchestrating a Best-in-class supply chain, maximizing customer value and enabling profitable growth.

The Product Lifecycle Planning Manager plays a pivotal role in realising this ambition. This is a high‑impact role for a Strategic Demand Planning Manager who thrives on complexity, influence, and driving transformation in collaborative, assumption-based forecasting for New Products. 

Sitting at the intersection of Product, Commercial, and Global Supply Chain, this role drives tight alignment between the product roadmap and supply chain strategies to ensure the right decisions are made at the right time—backed by insight, data, and cross‑functional partnership.

You will shape and execute product lifecycle strategies from new product launch through to end-of-life.  This includes enabling successful product launches, optimizing inventory for new and obsolescent products, and proactively managing risk while unlocking opportunities for profitable growth.

Working closely with cross‑functional stakeholders, you will lead demand and supply planning discussions for new and obsolescent products—challenging assumptions, improving forecast accuracy, and translating complexity into clear, actionable plans.

By leveraging advanced analytics, strong commercial acumen and deep expertise in Supply and Demand Planning you will deliver product lifecycle strategies that have a measurable impact in a dynamic and rapidly evolving MedTech landscape.

Let’s Talk About Responsibilities:

Strategic Lifecycle Planning

Lead the design and execution of end-to-end product lifecycle strategies that align with portfolio priorities, revenue growth, cost efficiencies, and competitiveness.

Ensure lifecycle decisions maximize customer value, safeguard availability, and support profitable growth.

Identify and mitigate supply chain risks to enable seamless new product introductions, scaling, and discontinuations.

Integrate commercial insights into demand forecasts with clear assumptions and robust review cycles.

Develop inventory strategies that balance availability with efficiency, including last-time buy planning for components tied to end-of-life products.

Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) to assess NPI forecast accuracy post launch

Review NPI Business Case and Ramp Plans and provide inputs into initial demand forecasts based on historical data, and inputs from Product teams including assumptions.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather relevant information, such as sales forecasts, inventory levels, and promotional activities, to enhance demand planning accuracy.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Build trusted partnerships with Commercial, Product, Project, and Supply Chain teams to deliver timely supply outcomes aligned with lifecycle strategies.

Drive alignment across functions on:

Supply chain and project risk management, with mitigation plans.

Demand assumptions, uncertainties, and changes.

Market dynamics and regional strategies.

Foster accountability with clear ownership through strong stakeholder engagement.

Communicate lifecycle plans and updates to business stakeholders, providing data-driven insights and recommendations.

Represent the supply chain function in product development reviews, project gate milestones, and commercial strategy sessions.

Commercial Acumen & Market Intelligence

Translate business strategy and customer needs into actionable planning initiatives.

Apply financial modelling, ROI analysis, and scenario planning to optimize lifecycle decisions.

Anticipate and adapt to market changes with data-driven insights.

Innovation & Process Optimization

Challenge the status quo with new ideas that drive transformation, cost optimization, and process excellence.

Partner with Planning Excellence to support Supply Chain initiatives.

Data-Driven Decision Making

Champion a data-centric approach to lifecycle planning by leveraging advanced analytics, predictive modelling, and real-time data from supply chain systems to inform decisions.

Confidently influence stakeholders by presenting evidence-based recommendations.

Success Metrics

On-time NPI delivery with full supply chain readiness.

Smooth execution of end-of-life and obsolescence plans without disruption.

Optimized inventory and costs

Strong stakeholder feedback on influence and collaboration.

Proactive lifecycle risk identification and mitigation.

Contributions to process improvements and digital supply chain initiatives.

Let’s Talk About You:

Education: Bachelor’s in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or related field (MBA preferred).

Experience: 5+ years in supply chain planning with strong demand planning and product lifecycle management experience and at least 2 years in MedTech or regulated healthcare.

Expertise in forecasting methodologies and tools.

Commercial Acumen: Strong financial analysis, forecasting, and P&L alignment.

Analytical Skills: Skilled in scenario modelling, trend analysis, and visualization tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau).

Technical Skills: Proficiency in ERP (SAP, Oracle), planning tools, and data analytics platforms.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and drive continuous improvement initiatives.

Detail-oriented with a strategic mindset, capable of balancing short-term needs with long-term objectives.

Soft Skills: Exceptional communication, stakeholder management, and influencing skills across all levels of the business.

Preferred: APICS CPIM/CSCP or PMP certification, agile product development experience, and exposure to sustainable or digital supply chain initiatives.

Why Join Us?

At ResMed, we believe in “Do it Best, Do it Together.” You’ll join a collaborative, forward-thinking team shaping the next generation of supply chain planning — from advanced demand planning to ERP transformation. Together, we are building a supply chain that is more reliable, agile, and impactful for patients worldwide.

#LI-APAC

Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! We commit to respond to every applicant.

Job Title: Applications Support Senior Analyst (Assistant Vice President)
Ad Text:
Why Citi? Join a Global Bank that believes in its talent.

We’re looking for talented individuals to help shape the future of global banking. At Citi, we are intensely focused on driving innovation and productivity. You’ll join a diverse global team and have the opportunity to build the career you want, with the scale, inclusive culture, and technology to support your progress.

About the Team: Data Solutions Production Management

Our Production Management team provides critical business and technical support for the Data Solutions applications, ensuring our platforms and services are always available for our clients. We are highly skilled and valued partners to the businesses we support.

Working as part of our team, you will help deliver a world-class client experience by applying engineering, innovation, and risk management across our systems. We collaborate daily to solve complex issues and proactively prevent future problems through diligent systems management.

We are a forward-thinking, collaborative team looking for ambitious professionals who want to improve how things are done. In return, we offer rewarding work in a supportive environment with clear opportunities for progression.

Role and Position Overview

As an Application Support Senior Analyst, you will take a central role in running and maintaining the production environment for Citi's critical Reference Data Applications – high visibility applications forming the backbone of Citi’s operations, serving hundreds of partner systems across Citi’s main businesses – Markets, Services, Banking, and Functions – enabling them to service our clients.

You will join a high-performing team focused on building a robust Production Support platform using the latest technologies, AI-driven automation, and reliability techniques. Our mission is to deliver a world-class client experience by digitizing services, eliminating manual work, and ensuring our platforms can scale for growth.

In this global role, you will provide support for our enterprise-critical applications, partnering closely with Application Support, Development, Infrastructure, Business, and Operations teams to ensure seamless service delivery.

Functional Key Responsibilities

Provide technical and business support for Citi users, addressing daily queries and issues, driving stability, efficiency, and risk management improvements to help the department and overall business succeed.

Provide resolution to critical incidents including prioritization, timely escalation and communication to key stakeholders, driving immediate fixes, as well as comprehensive post-mortems and root cause analysis of all problems for recurrence prevention.

Support the planning and execution of system changes, including application releases, infrastructure maintenance, and continuity of business tests, ensuring production stability is maintained.

Maintain and optimize the production monitoring/observability estate by implementing new features and leveraging analytics to enhance system visibility and proactive alerting.

 Collaborate closely with development teams to recommend and implement improvements to application stability, performance, and recoverability.

Identify opportunities and implement automation tools to reduce operational toil, improve efficiency, and mitigate risk.

Required Skills and Experience

5+ years in a Production Support role managing enterprise-level applications.

Deep expertise in Unix/Linux and advanced Shell scripting

Proficiency with enterprise monitoring tools (e.g., ITRS Geneos, AppDynamics) and log aggregation platforms (e.g., Splunk, ELK)

Experience with containerization platforms (OpenShift, Kubernetes)

Experience with relational (Oracle, MSSQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB) databases

Strong knowledge of messaging solutions (Tibco EMS, MQ, Kafka)

Experience working with REST APIs

Infrastructure: Solid understanding of distributed application architecture, including networks, load balancers, storage, and authentication (AD/LDAP)

Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical issues to both technical and business audiences.

Desirable Skills

Proficiency in a programming language (e.g., Python, Java) for automation.

Familiarity with prompt engineering techniques for interacting with Generative AI / Large Language Models (LLMs).

Experience with observability standards and tools: Open Telemetry, Prometheus, Grafana

Development Value

The Data Production Management organization follows best-in-class application support procedures, technical solutions, and risk & controls practices. You will be exposed to advanced technologies used to build Citi’s Reference Data Applications. The role presents an excellent opportunity for the successful candidate to develop a broad range of technical and soft skills. Data Solutions is a central function of Citi. The successful candidate will have an opportunity to develop extensive institutional knowledge by working in a rich environment, interfacing with virtually all businesses and functions across the bank.

Education

Bachelor’s/University Degree or equivalent experience will be considered.

We have more than 15 main sites, including hospitals, centres and clinics that provide comprehensive care from welcoming newborns to supporting older people and a full range of services in between.

More than 22,000 dedicated staff are focussed on providing exceptional, equitable, and locally connected care through shared expertise, compassion, and a commitment to continuous growth. Education and training are central to staff development as we encourage all employees to strive and thrive.

Bayside Health was formed following the merger of Alfred Health, Bass Coast Health, Gippsland Southern Health Service, Kooweerup Regional Health Service and Peninsula Health on 1 January 2026.

The Team & Role

The Finance Analyst position sits within the Finance and Commercial Directorate, which provides organisation wide leadership in financial stewardship, performance management, compliance and strategic resource planning. The role operates specifically within the Performance Analysis Reporting Unit (PARU), a specialist analytics and reporting function responsible for delivering high quality financial analysis, performance insights and data-driven reporting frameworks that support executive and operational decision-making. PARU plays a key role in strengthening organisational transparency, accountability and performance monitoring by integrating financial, activity and operational data into meaningful intelligence to guide both strategic priorities and day-to-day decision-making.

Position Summary

The Finance Analyst is a senior analytical role, operating with a high degree of professional autonomy and judgement to support financial sustainability and performance. The role provides advanced financial modelling, performance analysis, budgeting insight and activity-based evaluation to support strategic and operational decision-making across the Peninsula Care Group.

Working within PARU, the role strengthens organisational financial sustainability through high-level analytical expertise, enhanced reporting frameworks and robust planning tools. The position collaborates closely with Finance Business Partners, Activity Based Funding and Activity Based Costing team, Financial Reporting and operational leaders, to deliver accurate, timely and actionable financial insights that support organisational performance, accountability and continuous improvement.

Please click here to see the position description.






Skills & Experience Required

Apply advanced professional expertise to interpret complex financial, operational and activity-based data, delivering insights that inform high-level planning and resource allocation.
Lead development and enhancement of forecasting, costing, workforce and activity-based models using advanced Excel, Power Query, Power BI and enterprise finance systems.
Provide senior analytical support for annual budgets, forecasting cycles, performance targets and long-term financial sustainability initiatives.
Prepare high-quality financial and activity performance reports that support executive, board and directorate decision-making.
Undertake complex analysis to support activity-based funding allocation, efficiency reviews, value-for-money assessments and service evaluation.
Consolidate and synthesise insights from Finance Business Partners and system data sources to produce comprehensive performance narratives.
Provide expert advice on financial governance, system optimisation, data quality improvement and reporting methodologies within PARU.
Support Finance Business Partners and Senior Management on business cases, assessment of proposed investments, service changes, and business improvement projects through high-level analytical modelling.
Strengthening business intelligence and data capability across the Peninsula Care Group by improving data structures, reports, dashboards, templates and analytical workflows.
Key Capabilities

Analytical and Technical Expertise

Demonstrated ability to interpret complex, multi-source data sets and translate them into strategic insights.
Expert proficiency in:
Advanced Excel (complex formulas, Power Query, data modelling, automation).
Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development).
Power Query / ETL processes
Enterprise finance and reporting systems

Strategic and Conceptual Thinking

Ability to integrate financial, operational and activity-based insights into strategic recommendations that influence executive decision-making.

Professional Judgement and Autonomy

Demonstrated capacity to exercise independent judgment, resolve complex analytical issues and operate with minimal direction, consistent with the classification level for this role.

Influential Communication

Ability to present complex financial information clearly and persuasively to executives, clinical leaders, managers and governance committees.

Continuous Improvement and Systems Thinking

Commitment to improving analytic maturity, data governance, reporting frameworks and financial planning processes across the organisation.

Our client is a large, commercially focused utilities company with a strong reputation in the Canberra market.
 
They are an employer of choice in the Canberra region due to their high-performance culture, flexible working arrangements and employee benefits.
 
The Opportunity

There is an opportunity for a Senior Business Planning Officer to join the team, commencing before 1 May 2026 on a 6-month labour hire contract with a potential 6-month extension.
 
The organisation is in its first year of delivering its 10‑year business strategy and is seeking an experienced Business Planning Officer to support the development of the 2026–27 business plans and associated strategy implementation activities.
 
This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in translating strategy into practical, business-ready plans within a collaborative and fast-paced environment. There is flexibility to work from home up to 3 days per week, however you must be able to attend a series of face-to-face workshops scheduled throughout May 2026.
 
The Role

Your key responsibilities will include:

Support the planning, facilitation and documentation of organisation-wide business planning workshops

Deep expertise in Master Data Management (MDM), including customer, address, and reference data domains
Strong technical and architectural capability, with experience in enterprise data platforms and semantic modelling
Proven product ownership mindset, driving roadmaps, backlogs, prioritisation, and value delivery
Handson experience leading platform migrations (e.g. Informatica to Atacama) with minimal business disruption
Ability to influence, communicate, and drive adoption across technical and nontechnical stakeholders
Strategic thinking combined with a pragmatic, delivery-focused approach in fastpaced environments
A collaborative, proactive, and continuous improvement mindset aligned with enterprise transformation goals

Job Title: ERP Solutions Platform Lead - Corporate Services
Ad Text:
This is a high-impact, internal-facing role at the center of how our business operates day to day. You'll be the trusted partner to our internal teams, ensuring our core enterprise platforms are stable, secure and continuously improving so people can do their best work, for our growing enterprise. With a strong blend of hands-on systems ownership and people leadership, you'll drive a responsive support model, embed service standards, and translate recurring business needs into practical platform enhancements that supports global operations.

You'll also work closely with solution and portfolio teams to ensure new initiatives transition smoothly into support, and you'll empower key users through self-service, knowledge transfer and clear governance.

What you'll be doing…

Own the inhouse platforms in scope including TechnologyOne Ci, CiAnywhere and DXP, plus integrations (incl. Salesforce CPQ/CRM) and TechnologyOne-based automation/micro-systems (models, registers, workflows)
Run best-practice support operations across key user support, review cycles, performance and security checks, incident management, continuous improvement and other cyclic activities
Build deep knowledge of current solutions and guide key users on current state, risks and improvement opportunities
Lead prioritised platform uplift activities, including Group 0 projects, release and upgrade processes
Enable self-service by establishing design/technical review of changes and transferring knowledge to power users
Strengthen the support model by triaging tickets effectively, routing work into portfolio delivery where needed, and embedding SLAs and KPIs to lift quality and productivitycale
Support solution delivery by defining a consistent support handover process for new initiatives and ensuring escalations follow agreed portfolio pathways
Lead and coach the team to manage escalations, uplift troubleshooting capability, deepen domain knowledge, and improve documentation and the knowledge base
Train and enable new starters and junior staff across local and remote regions, and deliver key user training where requested
Drive continuous improvement and compliance by challenging the status quo, improving efficiency and cost, and maintaining WHS and policy compliance
Occasional work outside core hours may be required

Your Talents…



Degree in Information Systems (or similar) plus strong hands-on experience supporting/configuring ERP, enterprise apps and integration platforms
Solid understanding of end-to-end corporate processes (HR, Sales, Consulting, Finance, IT) with mature communication skills to influence stakeholders at all levels
Desirable: experience leading dispersed teams; exposure to XLOne, Crystal Reports, Workflow and Web Services; knowledge of ITIL and security/privacy best practice; agile delivery/change leadership; tools like Monday.com, Jira and Confluence

Why Join us?

TechnologyOne is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer who values diversity and an inclusive workplace. We aim to recruit a diverse range of people with a diverse range of talents to help us deliver on our mission to better our community.

Our core values and culture unleash the boundless potential of creativity, innovation and the entrepreneurial spirit within our people.

When you experience Life at TechnologyOne you'll see that our team enjoys an extensive array of benefits, including foundation charity activities, which all cultivate a vibrant workplace our people are very proud to be involved with.

Benefits…



Competitive remuneration package
Access to our industry leading employee share plan
Salary packaging options
Up to 10 days of additional leave via our Purchase Leave program
Amazing Culture: 8 x Australian Business Awards "Employer of Choice" winner
We love our team events & celebrate diversity
Flexible hours: we are family-friendly and value life outside of work
Free gym membership in your region (or, if you are located at HQ, we have a new state of the art Gym onsite, exclusive and available 24/7).
Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site
Accelerate your growth with full access to LinkedIn Learning and a clear career framework that maps your path to success.

Who we are…

At TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE), we're more than Australia's largest top 50 ASX enterprise SaaS company; we're a team driven by purpose!

Our global SaaS ERP solution powers over 1,300 leading organisations, including government, education, health, and financial services. We take full responsibility for delivering outcomes - reducing time, cost, and risk; therefore, our customers can focus on what matters most.

For over 38 years, we've been helping communities and organisations thrive by making life simple through innovative technology. Ready to make a real difference? Join us and help shape the future of enterprise technology while building a career that matters.

Category:
Networks & Systems Administration (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Company description:



Queensland Rail's purpose is to provide a customer-focused, safe, reliable, on-time, value for money rail service that benefits the community, supports industry and is integrated with the public transport system. The organisation's vision is connecting communities through a modern, world-class rail service.



Job description:



You'll connect Queensland. We'll connect you to an amazing career.



Queensland Rail stands on the foundation of true connection, encompassing the relationships we foster within our teams and the communities we serve across the state. For over 150 years, we've been instrumental in linking people and places, and now we invite you to contribute to our proud legacy and shape a brighter, sustainable future.

Your Opportunity

Queensland Rail is seeking a Senior Network Capability Analyst to provide authoritative modelling and simulation expertise supporting the performance, resilience and future growth of the South East Queensland rail network.



In this role, you will lead complex network capability analysis across current and future operating states, providing evidence‑based advice that directly informs service planning, infrastructure investment and network change decisions. You will work closely with planning, operations, infrastructure and asset teams to translate system complexity into clear, defensible insights.



This is a technically senior role suited to an experienced rail modeller who can operate with autonomy, influence outcomes, and maintain high analytical standards in a complex environment.


Key Responsibilities:

Lead the development and maintenance of end‑to‑end rail network simulation models representing current and future network states
Deliver absolute capability and capacity assessments integrating infrastructure, signalling, rollingstock and timetable assumptions
Build, validate and stress‑test concept timetables, including major event and future service scenarios
Analyse and articulate network constraints related to signalling layouts, junctions, turnouts, stabling access and run times
Apply robust modelling methodologies to identify true performance limits rather than relying on static assumptions
Establish and uphold modelling data standards, assumptions and governance to ensure consistency and auditability
Translate complex modelling outputs into clear operational and strategic advice for senior stakeholders
Support long‑term planning and investment decisions through multi‑horizon modelling and scenario analysis
Act as a trusted technical authority, advising stakeholders on the appropriate use and limitations of modelling outputs





About you:

Proven experience in rail network modelling, simulation or capacity analysis within a complex operational environment
Strong analytical capability with experience managing, validating and interpreting large, interdependent datasets
Sound understanding of rail operations, signalling principles, timetabling and infrastructure interactions
Experience using rail simulation tools such as OpenTrack, TRENO Suite, or equivalent (highly desirable)
Ability to exercise engineering judgement, challenge assumptions and improve analytical quality
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence through clear, evidence‑based advice
Ability to work autonomously while collaborating effectively across multi‑disciplinary teams

For further details please view the Position Description



Benefits that Count


All that Queensland Rail achieves each day relies on the hard work and talent of our teams. That's why we offer a range of attractive benefits designed to help you bring your best, including:



Starting base salary $134,736 + 12.75% Superannuation
Hybrid work - with 50% work from home.
Enjoy free travel to work and discounted rail travel
QRI Lifestyle offers members access to over 2,000 exclusive discounts and services
Prioritise your physical, emotional and financial wellbeing with our range of free services
You and your family can get fit and have fun with access to over 380 gyms and pools with our Fitness Passport
Shape your rewarding career with clear pathways, innovative projects and opportunities for growth and leadership




Sounds great! What's next?

Please apply for the Senior Network Capability Analyst position via our website by clicking onto the "Apply" button by 11:59pm (GMT+10:00 Brisbane) 19th April 2026.




Supporting a Diverse Workforce

At Queensland Rail we are 1TEAM and our diverse workforce enables us to connect communities every day. We are committed to building inclusive and respectful workplaces for all our people, strengthened by our diversity and encourage applications from First Nations Peoples, all genders, those from culturally diverse backgrounds, LGBTIQ+ community and people with disability, of all ages and backgrounds.



We strive to provide a barrier free and equitable recruitment experience.



If you do require an adjustment during the recruitment process, please contact Theresa via the phone number or email below.



Applicants for roles within Queensland Rail will be subject to Alcohol and Other Drugs pre-employment testing if progressed through the recruitment processes. The recruitment and selection process for this position might require shortlisted applicants to undertake psychometric assessment, pre-employment health assessment and background checks that might also include criminal history and credit checks. For roles that have pre-employment qualifications, QR will also be required to do formal qualification verification processes to ensure qualifications align to QR's requirements.



To learn more about this Queensland Rail career opportunity please contact Theresa with your specific enquiry via Theresa.dickson@qr.com.au Please do not email your application. We can only accept applications received via our website.

Job Title: Senior Risk Analyst - Governance and Accountability (Finance)
Ad Text:
At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined, which is why we support the diverse needs of everyone and provide flexibility to help you thrive.

As one of Australia's largest superannuation funds, we're focused on delivering strong outcomes for our members. A strong risk culture underpins everything we do, and our Governance & Enablement team plays a critical role in supporting sustainable growth, resilience and regulatory confidence.


Your New Role

In this 9-month contract, you'll partner with business units across the Finance Domain to enable effective management of their business risk frameworks in line with the Fund's overall Risk Management Framework.

You'll act as a trusted advisor, embedding risk practices into day‑to‑day operations, supporting informed decision‑making and ensuring business risks, controls and obligations are clearly understood, monitored and reported.

Key duties include but are not limited to;

Partnering with designated business units to provide end‑to‑end business risk and control support.

Monitoring and reporting on the risk and control environment, keeping management informed of key issues and trends.

Supporting the identification, assessment and documentation of risks and controls within the GRC system, including incidents, control self‑assessments and action tracking.

Facilitating root cause analysis of incidents and issues, identifying control weaknesses and improvement opportunities.

Conducting control design and effectiveness testing as part of the control review program and following up on remediation actions.

Supporting the implementation of the Fund's Risk Management Framework, including risk appetite assessments and key risk indicators.

Developing clear risk reporting and dashboards for senior management, governance forums and committees.

Engaging with internal and external auditors and supporting the timely remediation of audit findings.


What You'll Need

Relevant tertiary qualifications in commerce, finance or a related discipline, with industry qualifications or accreditations considered beneficial.

Experience in operational risk, internal or external audit, or risk and control roles within a large financial services organisation.

Strong understanding of risk management frameworks and first, second or third line risk practices.

Exposure to risk and control identification, testing methodologies and incident management.

Highly developed analytical skills with strong attention to detail.

Clear written and verbal communication skills, with confidence engaging and influencing senior stakeholders.

A collaborative, proactive approach and the ability to manage competing priorities in a changing environment.

Experience with Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) systems will be highly regarded.


Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to supporting your development through ongoing learning, coaching and career opportunities across our growing organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and a blended working environment, and we're open to discussing flexibility that works for you.

We cultivate a workplace built on safety, respect and inclusiveness, where diversity is valued and everyone feels they belong.


What's Next

Apply now if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes, and you're excited by the opportunity to make a meaningful impact for millions of members.

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy:

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

AustralianSuper is committed to providing an inclusive recruitment experience. If you require reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please let our Talent Acquisition team know.

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

The Opportunity - M&A Analytics Manager/Senior Manager





Maven Partners are working closely with a leading professional services firm’s Transaction Analytics team is currently seeking dynamic and experienced professionals for Manager and Senior Manager roles within a growing practice. As part of an established and forward-thinking analytics team, you will combine project leadership with hands-on delivery, leveraging data analytics to support clients in making informed decisions during critical transactions.

Lead and manage analytics projects, ensuring timely delivery of high-quality insights to clients

Collaborate with clients to understand business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions

Analyse large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies that inform strategic decision-making

Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders, translating complex data into actionable insights

Support senior leadership with business development activities, including contributing to proposals, pitch materials, and client presentations

Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement

Stay up to date with industry trends and emerging technologies to enhance service offerings



Requirements

Relevant tertiary qualifications in accounting, business, finance or economics, with a professional accreditation (or international equivalent) preferred

Strong commercial acumen and strategic mindset, with experience across areas such as M&A, strategy, management consulting, corporate finance or accounting

Advanced quantitative and analytical capability, with experience working with large datasets and translating business questions into robust analysis

Advanced proficiency in a leading data analytics platform is essential, as it is the primary tool used to deliver core analytics outputs

Experience with SQL, Python and/or R is highly advantageous, supporting bespoke analysis, coaching of team members, and effective review and troubleshooting

Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to clearly articulate insights through a commercial lens, influence senior stakeholders, and lead teams and projects effectively


Maven Partners

Maven Partners is a specialist financial advisory, accounting and taxation recruitment services business. The partners have a wealth of experience in executive search and interim management, servicing clients across Asia Pacific, from our offices in Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane.

At Maven Partners, we are holders and seekers of knowledge. In our case this refers to knowledge gained through experience, dedication, and perseverance. Knowledge is Maven's central value. 

To discuss this opportunity further please email jackpayne@mavenpartners.net to arrange a call and/or meeting with Jack.

$145k–$155k + Super 
Sydney (Mascot) | Hybrid WFH - Drivers Licence required 
6 Month FTC

You’ll work hands‑on across Power BI, SQL and modern data platforms to create clean, fast, reliable BI solutions. If you enjoy turning complex data into simple, intuitive insights, this role is for you.

Write strong SQL to extract, transform and validate data from multiple sources.
Apply best‑practice UX and visual design to deliver clear, engaging reporting.
Work with stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into BI solutions.

What you’ll need
